fr:title.text: Internal categories as models of a theory

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Internal categories as models of a theory

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The notion of a (meta-)category is an essentially algebraic theory. As such it is possible to compute models of this theory in any category that has finite limits.
Let E be a category with finite limits; then an internal category in E is defined by the following data:

fr:ol.fr:li: an object of objects C_{0}\in E,
an object of morphisms C_{1}\in E,
source and target morphisms s,t:C_{1}\to C_{0},
a generic identity morphism C_{0}\to C_{1},
a generic composition morphism C_{1}\times _{C_{0}}C_{1}\to C_{1},
satisfying a number of laws corresponding to those of a category.

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: For the details of these laws, we refer to any standard source.
